confirmed.title: DateColumn::displayFormat() does not exist
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace DateColumn `displayFormat(string)` with the real setters `date(string)` / `dateTime(string)` (plus `since()`, `timezone(string)`) in reference/php/table.md:33 and fix every runnable example (e.g. tutorial-first-crud.md:179 -> ->dateTime('d/m/Y H:i'), from-nova/from-filament -> ->date('d/m/Y')) across en/es/pt-BR.
confirmed.detail: The table reference lists DateColumn's setters as `displayFormat(string)`, `timezone(string)`. The actual class packages/table/src/Columns/DateColumn.php has NO displayFormat() method (and no __call magic, and no inherited displayFormat on the base Column). Its real formatting setters are date(string $format='Y-m-d'), dateTime(string $format='Y-m-d H:i:s'), since(), and timezone(string). A call to DateColumn::make('x')->displayFormat(...) would fatal. This same non-existent method appears in runnable code examples across the corpus: guide/tutorial-first-crud.md:179, guide/resources.md:130, guide/tables-forms.md:30, guide/migration/from-nova.md:149, guide/migration/from-filament.md:153 — and their es/ and pt-BR/ mirrors. (Note: DateField in the fields package DOES have displayFormat(), so the fields.md rows are correct — only the DateColumn/table usages are wrong.)
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. apps/docs/reference/php/table.md:33 lists DateColumn's extra setters as `displayFormat(string)`, `timezone(string)`. The real class packages/table/src/Columns/DateColumn.php (final, extends Column) defines its formatting setters as date(string='Y-m-d'), dateTime(string='Y-m-d H:i:s'), since(), and timezone(string) — there is NO displayFormat() method. The base packages/table/src/Column.php has no displayFormat() and no __call magic (only make() and standard setters), so DateColumn::make('x')->displayFormat(...) would fatal with an undefined-method error. The `timezone(string)` part of the doc row is correct, but `displayFormat(string)` is objectively false/misleading. The doc is in-scope (apps/docs/, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated) and the finding is not in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json (no displayFormat entry; the prior table.md ledger entries concern column/filter counts, not this row).

confirmed.title: BadgeColumn setter is icons(array), not icon(string)
confirmed.suggested_fix: Change `icon(string)` to `icons(array)` in the BadgeColumn row of reference/php/table.md:31 (and the es/pt-BR mirrors).
confirmed.detail: The reference table lists BadgeColumn's extra setters as `colors(array)`, `icon(string)`. packages/table/src/Columns/BadgeColumn.php only defines colors(array $map) and icons(array $map) — there is no singular icon(string) method (nor one on the base Column). The icon setter takes a value->icon map (plural).
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides. Doc apps/docs/reference/php/table.md:31 lists BadgeColumn extra setters as `colors(array)`, `icon(string)`. The source packages/table/src/Columns/BadgeColumn.php (final class, extends TextColumn) defines exactly two setters: `colors(array $map): static` (line 23) and `icons(array $map): static` (line 33) — both take a value->X map array. There is NO singular `icon(string)` method on BadgeColumn, nor on TextColumn, nor on the base Column.php (grep `public function icon(` across packages/table/src returns zero hits). The class docblock itself describes "value->colour and value->icon maps". So the doc's `icon(string)` is objectively false on two counts: the method name is plural `icons` and its parameter is an array map, not a string. In-scope (apps/docs, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated), checkable, and not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json (no badge entry).

confirmed.title: IconColumn setters icon()/color() do not exist
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace the IconColumn setters in reference/php/table.md:35 with the real ones: `options(array)`, `size(string)` (and update es/pt-BR mirrors).
confirmed.detail: The reference lists IconColumn's setters as `icon(string|Closure)`, `color(string|Closure)`. packages/table/src/Columns/IconColumn.php defines neither — its actual setters are options(array $map) and size(string). Calling ->icon() or ->color() on an IconColumn would fatal.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. apps/docs/reference/php/table.md:35 lists IconColumn extra setters as `icon(string|Closure)`, `color(string|Closure)`. The source packages/table/src/Columns/IconColumn.php (final class extends Column) defines ONLY options(array $map): static and size(string): static — no icon() and no color(). The Column abstract (Column.php) has no __call magic method and no inherited icon()/color() (grep for __call/function icon/function color returned empty). Therefore calling ->icon() or ->color() on an IconColumn would fatal with an undefined-method error. The doc is in-scope (apps/docs/reference, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated), the claim is objectively checkable, and it genuinely contradicts current code. Not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json (no IconColumn or table.md:35 entry).

confirmed.title: RelationshipColumn has no 3-arg make(name, relation, attribute) factory
confirmed.suggested_fix: In reference/php/table.md:37 replace `factory make(name, relation, attribute)` with `make(name)` + `->display(attribute)` (and update es/pt-BR mirrors).
confirmed.detail: The reference describes RelationshipColumn as `factory make(name, relation, attribute)`. The base Column::make is `make(string $name)` (single arg) and the constructor is `final public function __construct(string $name)`, so no subclass can add a 3-arg make. packages/table/src/Columns/RelationshipColumn.php configures the displayed attribute via the instance method display(string $attribute) — i.e. usage is RelationshipColumn::make('name')->display('attribute'), not a 3-argument factory.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. Doc apps/docs/reference/php/table.md:37 lists `RelationshipColumn | Eager-loaded relation | factory make(name, relation, attribute)`. The base `Arqel\Table\Column` (packages/table/src/Column.php) defines `final public function __construct(string $name)` (line 74) and `public static function make(string $name): static` (line 80) — single-arg only, and the constructor is final so no subclass can introduce a 3-arg make. packages/table/src/Columns/RelationshipColumn.php has NO make() override; it sets the displayed attribute via the instance method `display(string $attribute)` (line 29) and walks the relation by the column's own `name`. There is no separate `relation` argument. Actual usage is `RelationshipColumn::make('name')->display('attribute')`. The doc's claim of a 3-arg factory make(name, relation, attribute) is objectively false. Doc is in-scope (apps/docs reference, not PLANNING/tickets/changelog/generated), claim is checkable, and no ledger match exists.

confirmed.title: core README lists non-existent Artisan command arqel:action
confirmed.suggested_fix: Remove `, `arqel:action`` from the list (keep arqel:install, arqel:resource, arqel:field), or replace it with a command that actually ships, e.g. `arqel:widget`.
confirmed.detail: The line reads `Comandos Artisan: `arqel:install`, `arqel:resource`, `arqel:field`, `arqel:action``. The first three are real commands on disk (packages/core/src/Commands/InstallCommand.php = arqel:install, MakeResourceCommand.php = arqel:resource, packages/fields/src/Commands/MakeFieldCommand.php = arqel:field), but there is NO arqel:action command anywhere. A repo-wide grep for the signature string 'arqel:action' across all packages/* and packages-js/* PHP and TS sources returns zero matches (exit 1); no Console/Command class registers it, and the actions package (packages/actions/src) ships no Console commands at all. The doc claims a command that does not exist. (Distinct from the already-swept 'no Resource::actions() method' item — this is an Artisan generator command name, not a Resource method.)
confirmed.why_real: Verified both sides on disk. packages/core/README.md:19 reads: "Comandos Artisan: `arqel:install`, `arqel:resource`, `arqel:field`, `arqel:action`". The first three are real: packages/core/src/Commands/InstallCommand.php (arqel:install), MakeResourceCommand.php (arqel:resource), and packages/fields ships MakeFieldCommand.php (arqel:field). But arqel:action does not exist: a repo-wide PHP grep for "arqel:action" across packages/* and packages-js/* returns zero matches (exit 1). The actions package (packages/actions/src) contains Action.php, Actions.php, ActionsServiceProvider.php, Concerns, Http, Types — no Console/Commands directory and no command class. The only "arqel:action" string in the whole repo is in packages-js/mcp-server/planning/05-api-php.md, a forward-looking PLANNING doc, not a registered command. The README is in-scope (a package README, not PLANNING/tickets/superpowers/CHANGELOG/generated), the claim is concretely checkable, and it genuinely contradicts the code. Not in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json (no arqel:action or core/README entry). Distinct from the already-swept Resource::actions() method finding — that is an Eloquent/Resource method, this is an Artisan generator command name.

confirmed.title: core README lists Artisan commands arqel:field and arqel:action that do not exist
confirmed.suggested_fix: Replace the command list with the commands core actually registers, e.g. '`arqel:install`, `arqel:resource`, `arqel:make-user`, `arqel:doctor`, `arqel:audit`, `arqel:introspect`' (drop arqel:field and arqel:action).
confirmed.detail: The 'Posição no ecossistema' section claims core provides 'Comandos Artisan: `arqel:install`, `arqel:resource`, `arqel:field`, `arqel:action`'. On disk, packages/core/src/Commands/ contains only InstallCommand (arqel:install), MakeResourceCommand (arqel:resource), and MakeUserCommand (arqel:make-user); ArqelServiceProvider.php registers InstallCommand, MakeResourceCommand, MakeUserCommand, DoctorCommand, IntrospectCommand, AuditCommand, CloudInfoCommand, PulseInfoCommand. There is no MakeFieldCommand/MakeActionCommand and no 'arqel:field' or 'arqel:action' signature anywhere in src/ (grep returns nothing). The two named commands are nonexistent.
confirmed.why_real: packages/core/README.md:19 (in-scope: a package README, not PLANNING/tickets/CHANGELOG/generated) claims core provides Artisan commands `arqel:install`, `arqel:resource`, `arqel:field`, `arqel:action`. Verified the contradicting reality on disk: packages/core/src/Commands/ contains only InstallCommand, MakeResourceCommand, MakeUserCommand. ArqelServiceProvider.php registers 8 commands (Install, MakeResource, MakeUser, Doctor, Introspect, Audit, CloudInfo, PulseInfo) — none named MakeField/MakeAction. grep for 'arqel:field', 'arqel:action', 'MakeFieldCommand', 'MakeActionCommand' across src/ returns nothing. So while arqel:install and arqel:resource are real, arqel:field and arqel:action are nonexistent, making the documented list objectively false/misleading. Not present in docs-fixed-ledger.json (grep exit 1).

confirmed.suggested_fix: Remove 'Renderable' from the contracts list so it reads 'Contracts (`HasResource`, `HasFields`, `HasActions`, `HasPolicies`)'.
confirmed.detail: The section lists 'Contracts (`HasResource`, `HasFields`, `HasActions`, `HasPolicies`, `Renderable`)'. packages/core/src/Contracts/ contains only HasActions.php, HasFields.php, HasPolicies.php, HasResource.php — there is no Renderable contract, and grep -rn 'Renderable' over packages/core/src/ returns no match. The 'Renderable' entry is a phantom contract.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed both sides. packages/core/README.md:15 lists contracts "(HasResource, HasFields, HasActions, HasPolicies, Renderable)". On disk, packages/core/src/Contracts/ contains only HasActions.php, HasFields.php, HasPolicies.php, HasResource.php — the four named-and-existing match, but there is NO Renderable contract. grep -rn 'Renderable' over packages/core/src/ returns zero matches, so it is not defined elsewhere in the package either. The README is in-scope (not PLANNING/tickets/changelog/generated) and the claim is checkable. Not present in reports/docs-fixed-ledger.json (grep exit 1). The 'Renderable' entry is a phantom/broken reference — the doc statement is objectively false.
